section_1_business_information.how_did_you_hear_about_us: I first heard about Cossio Insurance Agency through a discussion thread on the Airsoft Society online forum in early 2024. Several field owners there mentioned that "CIA" specializes in airsoft and paintball facilities and understands the unique risk profile better than generalist agents. After that, I looked up more information on Facebook groups like "Airsoft Field Owners of America" and saw your name come up multiple times in recommendations. I then visited your website and watched a YouTube interview with another field owner who talked about how easy it was to get set up with your airsoft program. Based on those recommendations, I decided to reach out for a quote.
section_1_business_information.detailed_description_of_operations:
Frontline Tactical Airsoft LLC operates an outdoor airsoft recreation park and training facility under the DBA "Frontline Tactical Airsoft Park" in Colorado Springs, Colorado. We primarily host walk-on recreational airsoft games for the general public on weekends, as well as private group events such as birthday parties, bachelor parties, corporate team-building days, and occasionally ROTC / JROTC training exercises.

Our facility consists of approximately 18 acres of mixed terrain, including wooded areas with natural cover, a small ravine, and an open field that has been developed into a mock urban environment with plywood structures, shipping containers, and constructed bunkers. We run a variety of game formats, including team deathmatch, capture the flag, VIP escort, and objective-based milsim-style scenarios. All gameplay is non-lethal and uses 6mm plastic BBs fired from airsoft replica guns that are powered by electric, gas, or spring mechanisms.

We maintain strict safety protocols: all players and referees are required to wear full-seal ANSI-rated eye protection at all times on the field, and players under 18 must also wear lower face protection. We run mandatory safety briefings before every session covering field rules, safe weapon handling, engagement distances, blind fire prohibitions, and emergency procedures. We chronograph all airsoft guns before they enter play and enforce maximum FPS and joule limits depending on the type of gun and role (e.g., 1.5J for standard rifles, slightly higher for bolt-action sniper with minimum engagement distances clearly explained and enforced). Any guns exceeding our limits are not allowed on the field.

We average between 30–80 players on a typical Saturday or Sunday, depending on weather and time of year, with peak season being April through October. We have 8–10 part-time referees and field staff who supervise games, enforce rules, and respond to minor injuries (such as twisted ankles, scrapes, or minor welts). We keep a fully stocked first-aid kit on site, maintain incident logs, and have a written emergency action plan, including evacuation routes and designated meeting points. In the event of more serious injuries, we call 911 and coordinate with local EMS.

We also operate a small on-site pro shop out of a converted shipping container near the staging area. The shop sells airsoft BBs, basic tactical gear (goggles, face masks, gloves, chest rigs), rental guns, batteries, and accessories. We do not sell real firearms or live ammunition. We rent airsoft guns and safety gear to customers who do not own their own equipment. Rental packages include full-face protection and mandatory barrel covers while off the field.

In addition to recreational play, we occasionally host pre-approved training events for local security teams and church safety teams who want to practice communications, movement, and de-escalation in a controlled, non-lethal force-on-force environment. These events are supervised by our senior staff and follow the same safety standards as our public games.

Our operating hours are typically Saturdays and Sundays from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, with some extended evening games during summer months and special events such as 8–12 hour milsim days once per quarter. We also open by reservation on select weekdays for private groups of 20 or more. We do not allow overnight camping at this time.

We maintain general site maintenance, including regular mowing of high-traffic areas, trimming of low-hanging branches, and inspection of all structures for stability (e.g., reinforcing platforms, removing protruding nails, and replacing damaged plywood). We have clearly marked parking areas, staging zones, and safe zones where magazines must be removed and barrel covers installed. Alcohol is strictly prohibited on the premises during operating hours, and anyone suspected of being under the influence of drugs or alcohol is not allowed to play.

Overall, our operations focus on providing a safe, structured, and family-friendly environment for airsoft enthusiasts of various experience levels, with a strong emphasis on safety procedures, supervision, and clear field rules.

section_2_insurance_and_property_informa.incidents_or_claims_last_5_years_explana: We have not had any formal insurance claims or reportable incidents in the last 5 years. Since opening in 2022, we have experienced only minor first-aid situations such as small cuts, scrapes, and ankle twists that were handled on-site with basic first aid and did not require EMS transport or medical treatment beyond self-care. None of these events resulted in lawsuits, formal complaints, or any interaction with an insurance carrier. We keep a written incident log for internal tracking, but to date nothing has risen to the level of an insurance claim.
section_2_insurance_and_property_informa.address_lessor_landlord: 6120 Old Ranch Road, Suite 200, Colorado Springs, CO 80923
